description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a sleek, elongated fuselage painted primarily in black with distinctive white fern motifs and the 'AIR NEW ZEALAND' branding along the side. The wings are long, swept back, and feature upward-curved winglets at the tips, contributing to aerodynamic efficiency. Two large turbofan engines are mounted under the wings, one on each side, and the landing gear is visible beneath the fuselage, indicating a tricycle retractable configuration. The tail section has a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer adorned with the airline's logo and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The overall appearance is modern and streamlined, suitable for long-haul commercial flights.
description: A modern, black Air New Zealand passenger airplane with swept-back wings, underwing turbofan engines, and a conventional tail. The fuselage features white fern motifs and prominent airline branding.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from above, highlighting the full wingspan, upward-curved winglets, and the black fuselage with white accents.
description_view_1: A left-side profile view shows the 'AIR NEW ZEALAND' branding, white fern motif, and the underwing engines clearly.
description_view_2: A front view emphasizes the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: A right-side profile view mirrors the left, displaying the airline logo on the tail and the overall streamlined shape.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with long, swept-back wings and upward-curved winglets at the tips.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows for pilot visibility.
